## 🌾 Our Pledge

Farm Intellect exists for **one reason**: to put the power of AI in the hands of every Indian farmer — regardless of language, caste, gender, religion, or income.

This community reflects that mission. We, as members, contributors, and leaders, pledge to make participation in our project a **harassment-free, inclusive, and empowering experience** for everyone — whether you're a farmhand in Vidarbha or a developer in Bengaluru.

We pledge to act and interact in ways that contribute to an open, welcoming, diverse, and healthy community.

---

## ✅ Our Standards

### 💚 Encouraged Behavior

- 🤝 **Use welcoming and inclusive language** — across all 22 languages we support
- 🌱 **Be respectful of differing viewpoints** — a cotton farmer and a software engineer may see the same problem differently. Both are valid.
- 📣 **Give and gracefully accept constructive feedback**
- 🎯 **Focus on what is best for the community** — especially the farmers we serve
- 💬 **Show empathy towards other community members**
- 🙏 **Credit others' contributions** — especially those from underrepresented communities
- 🌍 **Celebrate diversity** — language, culture, profession, background

### ❌ Unacceptable Behavior

- 🚫 Sexualized language or imagery, or unwelcome sexual attention or advances
- 🚫 Trolling, insulting/derogatory comments, or personal/political attacks
- 🚫 Public or private harassment of any kind
- 🚫 Publishing private information of others (doxxing) without permission
- 🚫 Discrimination based on — caste, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, age, language, or socioeconomic status
- 🚫 Dismissing contributions because someone is "just a farmer" or "not a real developer"
- 🚫 Any conduct reasonably considered inappropriate in a professional setting

---

## 🏛️ Enforcement Responsibilities

Project maintainers are responsible for clarifying and enforcing these standards. They will:

- Take appropriate, fair corrective action in response to any behavior deemed harmful
- Remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, and other contributions that violate this Code of Conduct
- Communicate reasons for moderation decisions when appropriate

---

## 🔭 Scope

This Code of Conduct applies in **all project spaces**, including:

| Space | Examples |
|:--|:--|
| 💻 Online | GitHub Issues, PRs, Discussions, code reviews |
| 💬 Communication | Slack, Discord, email threads |
| 🌍 Public | Conferences, meetups, social media when representing the project |
| 👨‍🌾 Field | Any in-person farmer engagement done under the project banner |

---

## 📢 Reporting

> **Incidents can be reported confidentially.**

If you experience or witness unacceptable behavior, please report it:

| Channel | Details |
|:--|:--|
| 📧 **Email** | Open an issue marked `[PRIVATE]` or contact maintainers directly via GitHub |
| 🔒 **GitHub** | Use the "Report content" feature on any comment or issue |
| 💬 **Direct** | Message [@Samrudh2006](https://github.com/Samrudh2006) privately |

All reports will be:
- ✅ Reviewed promptly (within 72 hours)
- ✅ Kept confidential to the extent possible
- ✅ Acted upon fairly and consistently

---

## ⚖️ Enforcement Guidelines

Maintainers follow these Community Impact Guidelines when determining consequences:

### 1. 📝 Correction
**Impact:** Minor unprofessional language or behavior.
**Consequence:** Private written warning. Public apology may be requested.

### 2. ⚠️ Warning
**Impact:** A single incident or series of violations.
**Consequence:** Formal warning. No interaction with affected parties for a specified period. Violation leads to temporary ban.

### 3. ⏸️ Temporary Ban
**Impact:** Serious violation — sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment, or aggression.
**Consequence:** Temporary ban from all project interaction for a defined period.

### 4. 🚫 Permanent Ban
**Impact:** Pattern of violations, sustained harassment, or egregious single act.
**Consequence:** Permanent removal from all project spaces. No appeal.

---

## 🌍 Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/), version 2.1, available at [https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html).

Community Impact Guidelines were inspired by [Mozilla's code of conduct enforcement ladder](https://github.com/mozilla/diversity).
